adm_api_key_missing_thehive: API key: missing (set THEHIVE_API_KEY or THEHIVE_API_TOKEN)
adm_authz_intro_1: Tell the SOC what activity in your environment is authorized. Authorization comes in two forms: standing
adm_authz_intro_2: (an approved change, a service account's routine work) and time-boxed
adm_authz_intro_3: (an authorized pentest or red-team window). An engagement is simply a bounded authorization of attack-shaped activity.
adm_byo_llm_title: Bring your own LLM API key — investigations bill to you instead of your MSSP
adm_deactivate_confirm: Deactivate {email}? They will no longer be able to sign in.
adm_eng_empty: No engagements declared for this tenant yet.
adm_eng_intro: Declare authorized offensive activity against your own environment — a pentest or red-team window and its scope — so the SOC deconflicts it. A declared engagement adds context; it never suppresses detections, and activity outside its scope still escalates.
adm_fact_form_hint_1: Paste an authorization fact (JSON). It's submitted at low trust and lands
adm_facts_empty: No authorization facts for this tenant yet.
adm_facts_intro: Org-state the triage engine reasons over. Facts arrive from connectors (the ingest API), SIEM-derived routine, or analyst answers. Revoking is a soft delete; the audit row survives.
adm_llm_keys_conflict: Both API keys are set (choose exactly one).
adm_llm_restart_hint: Changes apply when the orchestrator (re)starts. Provider selection requires the matching API key in the environment.
adm_llm_section_hint: Provider and model preferences (API keys stay in env)
adm_modal_new_fact_hint: A typed AuthorizationFact (grant / prohibition / change_freeze / entity_context). Stored as analyst_asserted (trust 60).
adm_must_change_hint: Your administrator requires you to set a new password before continuing.
adm_my_facts_empty: No authorization facts for your organization yet.
adm_my_facts_intro: Anything you assert is reviewed by an analyst before it can affect triage, and it can never close an alert that carries a threat indicator.
adm_password_updated: Password updated. Redirecting to your dashboard…
adm_role_analyst_full: Analyst — operate the SOC (triage, review, decide, chat)
adm_select_tenant: Select a tenant to view its authorization facts.
adm_temp_password_hint: One-time temporary password — copy it now, it won't be shown again. They'll be asked to change it on first sign-in:
adm_toast_load_failed_msg: Failed to load settings from server.
adm_toast_reset_msg: Settings have been reset to defaults.
adm_toast_save_failed_msg: Failed to save settings. Please try again.
adm_users_intro: Provision logins for your organization and set what each person can do. An analyst runs the SOC; a viewer only watches; a manager authorizes risk; an admin configures the system and manages users.
ana_heatmap_hint: Day-of-week × hour-of-day, last {days} days. Darker = busier.
ana_mssp_subtitle: Is the service improving, degrading, or drifting? Trend-shaped cross-tenant view.
ana_no_tenants_min_sample: No tenants meet the minimum sample for this window.
cap_annotate_desc: Write a system note on the investigation (local, tier-0).
cap_external_desc: POST a named action to an operator-configured endpoint. Requires human approval.
cap_notify_desc: POST the signed envelope to the tenant webhook connector (tier-0).
chat_explainer: The AI SOC Analyst can summarise cases, dig through alerts and the audit log, and propose actions on pending reviews.
chip_all_tenants_title: Cross-tenant view — pick a tenant from /tenants to scope down
chip_clear_title: Clear tenant pin and return to cross-tenant view
cp_ask_fleet: Ask about any tenant. Use tenant slugs (e.g. "acme-corp") to scope queries.
dash_mssp_subtitle: Cross-tenant fleet view. Click a row to drill in.
dash_no_events_yet: No events yet. Events will appear here in real-time.
dash_no_open_fleet: No open investigations across the fleet.
dash_no_repeated_iocs: No IOCs seen in ≥2 tenants in the last {days} days.
flow_add_hint: add an action to see the flow
flow_approval_sub:
gated actions wait for an
analyst before they execute
inv_ask_ai_title: Ask the SOC AI about this investigation
inv_cancel_confirm: Are you sure you want to cancel this investigation?
llm_intro: Investigation runs use this credential to call your LLM provider. By default your service provider funds these calls on a shared install-wide key. Paste your own key below to bring your own account — your runs will be billed to you, not your MSSP.
llm_key_updated_msg: Your investigation runs will use this key starting with the next case.
llm_provider_hint: These are configured by your service provider and apply to all tenants on this install. Contact your MSSP if you need a different provider.
llm_revert_title_attr: Stop using your own key; resume on the MSSP shared credential.
llm_reverted_msg: Your runs will resume on your service provider’s shared LLM credential.
llm_shared_in_use: Your service provider's shared key is in use. Paste your own below to switch.
llm_storage_hint: Stored encrypted at rest in your service provider's control plane and mounted into your investigation worker only. Never visible to other tenants.
login_auth_disabled: Authentication is disabled for this deployment.
login_proxy_hint: This deployment uses upstream authentication. Sign in via your SSO provider and refresh.
rev_feedback_placeholder: Add your feedback, analysis notes, or reasoning...
rev_modal_request_hint: Add questions or requests for additional analysis. The system will gather more information and update the review.
rev_no_pending: No pending reviews at this time.
rev_toast_already_handled_msg: This review was already handled via Slack. Refreshing list...
rev_toast_question_required_msg: Please add at least one question
rp_confirm_delete: Delete response playbook "{id}"? This removes it from the tenant.
rp_intro: Procedural response dispatched after the triage disposition is final — the playbook names vetted capabilities per disposition. Tier-0 actions (annotate, notify) fire autonomously; higher-tier actions route to a human-approved proposal. Activate one to dispatch live; deactivate returns it to shadow (audited only).
rp_modal_hint: Definition (JSON). Validated server-side: fail-closed, vetted capability names only, on_close restricted to annotation-tier actions. New/edited playbooks land as shadow — activate to dispatch live.
rp_note_active: "{id}" is now active — it governs response dispatch immediately.
rp_note_shadow: "{id}" returned to shadow — audited only, no longer dispatched.
rp_pin_hint: Pin a tenant (from Tenants) to author response playbooks for it.
rpe_applies_hint: Match the triage envelope. Leave both empty to match every alert.
rpe_gated_hint: Gated: this action routes to a human-approved proposal before it executes.
rpe_not_found: response playbook '{id}' not found for this tenant
rpe_pin_hint: Pin a tenant to author response playbooks.
rpe_saved_shadow_note: Saved as shadow — activate from the list.
rpe_status_note: — edits keep the current status; activate/deactivate from the list.
rpe_tactic_hint: tactic names as your source emits them
sse_review_required_msg: A new investigation requires human review
ten_empty_hint: No tenants yet. Click {button} to onboard one.
ten_llm_chain_hint: Route a tier to a dedicated backend — e.g. a cheap self-hosted router feeding a frontier reasoning model. A dedicated backend supersedes the matching model override above.
ten_llm_clear_key_confirm_prompt: Clear the tenant API key and fall back to the MSSP shared install key?
ten_llm_clear_tier_key_label: Clear the tier key — reuse the primary credential
ten_llm_err_base_url: Base URL must start with http:// or https://
ten_llm_err_dollar_budget: Dollar budget must be at least 0.10 and at most 10000
ten_llm_err_max_tokens_range: Max tokens must be a whole number between 1 and 8192
ten_llm_err_temp_range: Temperature must be a number between 0 and 2
ten_llm_err_token_budget: Token budget must be a whole number between 1000 and 100000000
ten_llm_key_cleared: Tenant API key cleared — using MSSP shared install key
ten_llm_rollout_note: Provider, endpoint or model changes (including fast/thinking model overrides) roll out via a re-render of the tenant release; key-only changes apply within seconds.
ten_llm_tier_err_base_url: {tier} tier: base URL must start with http:// or https://
ten_llm_tier_err_cross_provider: {tier} tier is on a different provider ({provider}) than the primary ({primary}) and needs its own API key
ten_llm_tier_err_max_tokens: {tier} tier: max tokens must be a whole number 1–8192
ten_llm_tier_err_temp: {tier} tier: temperature must be between 0 and 2
ten_llm_use_primary_model_hint: leave blank to use the primary model
ten_open_soc_title: Pin session to this tenant — SOC pages will scope to them
ten_open_soc_title_not_ready: Tenant is {state} — wait for it to reach 'active' before pinning. Per-tenant SOC data isn't available yet.
tnew_api_token_hint: Optional pre-minted manager token; overrides password auth.
tnew_blank_primary_hint: leave blank to use the primary model
tnew_key_blank_hint: leave blank to use the MSSP shared install key
tnew_key_required_hint: Required for provided tenants — you'll confirm it on the External SIEM step.
tnew_llm_required_hint: Provided tenants use their own LLM API key — the MSSP shared install key does not apply to this profile.
tnew_persistent_desc: Single-node but durable. PVC-backed indexer + manager. No HA (deferred).
tnew_poc_desc: Ephemeral, single-node, node-local storage, no ingress, tight resource quotas. Right for demo tenants.
tnew_provided_desc: The tenant already runs Wazuh. SocTalk deploys only the adapter + runs-worker and points them at your external indexer + API. You'll enter the credentials in the next step.
tnew_provider_hint: leave on "install default" to inherit the MSSP's provider, model and shared key — the Base URL / Model above only apply once you pick a provider
tnew_siem_intro: SocTalk connects to your existing Wazuh. The Indexer (OpenSearch) and the API (manager) authenticate with separate credentials.
tnew_slug_hint: 3–32 chars, lowercase letters/digits/hyphens. Used in URLs and namespace.
tnew_submit_hint: Submitting kicks off namespace + chart provisioning. The tenant will land in
tp_active_revision_note: This policy currently governs triage — saving a revision keeps it active and queues a worker rollout with the new definition.
tp_allowed_supervisor_actions: Allowed supervisor actions per phase (unchecked phase = unconstrained)
tp_authored_intro: Triage policies for this tenant, validated server-side. Activate one to govern triage (queues a worker rollout); deactivate returns it to shadow. Export to YAML for git-managed rollout.
tp_authored_pin_hint: Pin a tenant (from Tenants) to author shadow triage policies for it.
tp_builder_unrepresentable_json_notice: this condition uses shapes the visual builder cannot show — editing as JSON
tp_builder_unrepresentable_keep_json_not: this condition uses shapes the visual builder cannot show (e.g. ! / !!) — keep editing it as JSON
tp_close_requires_signoff: Close requires human sign-off for data classes:
tp_close_signoff_label: A committing close on an asset with one of these data classifications waits for a human (comma-separated)
tp_condition_builder_any_title: At least one row below must hold
tp_confirm_delete: Retire triage policy "{id}"? This removes it from the tenant.
tp_contract_asset_criticality_help: Trust-resolved criticality of the activity’s asset.
tp_contract_asset_data_classification_he: Trust-resolved classification of the activity’s asset (free-form; common values suggested).
tp_contract_asset_environment_help: Trust-resolved environment of the activity’s asset.
tp_contract_authz_actor_genuine_help: No signal that the actor identity is compromised or spoofed.
tp_contract_authz_class_help: Engine ruling: covered (a record fully covers), contradicted (records present but do not cover), absent (no record of the right kind).
tp_contract_authz_in_scope_help: A single record fully covers the activity (actor, host, action, window).
tp_contract_authz_policy_allowed_help: No prohibition record forbids the action.
tp_contract_authz_sanctioned_or_routine_: The activity is sanctioned by a record or routine for this actor.
tp_contract_correlation_active_incident_: The investigation correlates to an open incident.
tp_contract_enrichment_ioc_help: A malicious enrichment verdict or a threat-intel IOC match.
tp_contract_fields_note: Contract fields simulated: {count}. The worker's guard remains the authority — this preview mirrors it for authoring feedback only.
tp_contract_verdict_confidence_help: Model confidence in the draft verdict (0–1).
tp_contract_verdict_help: The decision the model drafted — the guard runs before it commits.
tp_decision_flow_hint: Projection of this document onto the triage pipeline — click a guardrail to jump to it.
tp_deterministic_disposition_note: — closes without an LLM look unless a security veto fires
tp_editor_intro: Authored triage policies run in shadow: matched and evaluated for audit, enforcing nothing, until promoted. The safety floor (IOC / contradicted-authorization vetoes) always applies and cannot be weakened here — guardrails can only raise suspicion, never suppress it.
tp_editor_pin_hint: Pin a tenant (from Tenants) to author triage policies.
tp_flow_close_signoff_subtitle:
a close on a {classes} asset
waits for a human
tp_flow_llm_drafts_verdict_subtitle:
close · needs_more_info · escalate
(proposes — the guard disposes)
tp_flow_safety_floor_subtitle:
IOC present or contradicted authorization
caps any close at escalate (not editable)
tp_guardrail_reason_label: Reason shown to the analyst when this fires
tp_guardrails_hint: Evaluated after the safety floor, in order — the first matching rule wins. Overrides can only raise a decision (close → needs_more_info → escalate); interrupts hold the draft for human review.
tp_intro: Deterministic guardrails over the AI triage loop — the LLM proposes, a triage policy disposes. Shows the compiled-in (built-in) triage policies that govern triage; these are vetted code and read-only here.
tp_json_document_hint: The form and this JSON are the same document. Edit here and apply, or copy it out — the YAML export uses the identical structure.
tp_keep_as_draft: Keep as draft (not yet shadow-evaluated against live runs)
tp_load_authored_failed: Failed to load authored triage policies
tp_match_section_hint: Criteria are OR'd — the triage policy applies when any one matches.
tp_matching_only: Matching only — no gates or dispositions configured.
tp_modal_hint: Definition (JSON). Validated server-side: shadow-only, priority ≥ 60, no deterministic_disposition, id must not collide with a built-in, sandboxed guardrail conditions.
tp_not_found: triage policy '{id}' not found for this tenant
tp_required_steps_label: Steps that must run before a verdict is legal
tp_rollout_activating: Activating "{id}" — a worker rollout was queued; it governs once the reconcile completes.
tp_rollout_deactivating: Deactivating "{id}" — a worker rollout was queued.
tp_sim_reason_authz_floor: safety floor: authorization records present but do not cover
tp_sim_reason_commit: no guardrail fired — the draft stands
tp_sim_reason_ioc_floor: safety floor: malicious indicators never let a close stand
tp_sim_reason_signoff: close on a {dataClass}-classified asset requires human sign-off
tp_try_it_hint: Set a sample verdict context and see what this triage policy would do.
tp_validate_builtin_only: deterministic_disposition is a built-in-only capability and cannot be authored
tp_validate_close_not_grantable: CLOSE cannot be granted in phase '{phase}' — leave the phase unconstrained or route terminal decisions through VERDICT
tp_validate_condition_list_cap: list literals are capped at {max} entries
tp_validate_condition_list_scalars: list literals may contain only scalars
tp_validate_condition_root: a condition must be a mapping at its root
tp_validate_condition_single_mapping: each condition node must be a single {shape} mapping
tp_validate_field_not_contract: field '{field}' is not in the state contract
tp_validate_guardrail_effect: guardrail {n}: effect must be override or interrupt
tp_validate_guardrail_interrupt_target: guardrail {n}: interrupt guardrails route to human_review only
tp_validate_guardrail_override_target: guardrail {n}: override guardrails target a disposition, not review
tp_validate_guardrail_target: guardrail {n}: target must be escalate, needs_more_info or human_review
tp_validate_id_slug: id must be a slug: lowercase letters, digits, hyphens
tp_validate_max_guardrails: at most {max} guardrails per triage policy
tp_validate_priority_floor: priority must be an integer ≥ {floor} — built-in protections may not be outranked
tp_validate_var_string: var takes a single dotted field name string
verr_fix_json: fix invalid JSON in action params
verr_id_slug: id must be a slug: lowercase letters, digits, hyphens.
verr_need_action: add at least one action (on escalate or on close).
